claim: Serial execution of agent features is better than parallel; parallelism causes conflicts, duplication, and inconsistency
actionable: For multi-day agent workflows, use serial execution with internal parallelization only on read-only ops (search, review).
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Validation contracts written during planning (before code) define correctness independently of implementation
actionable: In planning phase, write assertions for what 'done' means; validation enforces these, not post-hoc tests written by implementer.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Three-role architecture (Orchestrator→Workers→Validators) with structured handoffs enables multi-day agent missions
actionable: Design agents with explicit roles: planner (sets requirements), executor (implements), validator (checks independently); use handoff logs.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Structured handoffs between agents must include: completed work, undone items, command history, exit codes, issues found
actionable: After each agent step, log structured handoff with: task completion status, problems encountered, next agent's entry requirements.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Validators should include both scrutiny (lint, tests, code review) and user testing (live app interaction); neither has seen code
actionable: Add two-stage validation: (1) automated checks (tests, linting), (2) adversarial agent interaction (simulated user behavior).
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Model selection per role ('droid whispering') is compounding advantage; different models excel at planning, coding, validation
actionable: Use slow reasoning models for planning, fast models for coding, precise instruction-following models for validation; test per-role defaults.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Agent architectures that define logic in prompts (not hard-coded state machines) improve with each model generation
actionable: Keep agent orchestration logic in prompts/skills; minimize deterministic code; enables automatic improvements with better models.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships

claim: Mission control dashboards show token burn, milestone progress, and agent handoff summaries for asynchronous monitoring
actionable: Build dashboards tracking: token spent vs budget, completed features, validation status, pending decisions for long-running agents.
source_title: The Multi-Agent Architecture That Actually Ships
